_meta: Plain-language narrative for the everyday Maui / Hawaiian person. build_site.add_narrative() injects the matching line at the top of each page (exact filename first, then longest prefix, then default). Keep each one warm, plain, and short.

exact.county_dashboard.html: This is the front door to watching Maui County — where the money goes, how your leaders vote, and what is coming up. Start here.
exact.accountability_record.html: A running record of what officials did, and whether it matched what they promised.
exact.sole_source_watch.html: Contracts the county handed out without open bidding. Sometimes there is a good reason — this page lets you check for yourself.
exact.patterns_money_x_votes.html: When the same names that gave money also got votes or contracts, the pattern shows up here. We ask it as a question, never an accusation.
exact.contracts_x_donors.html: A side-by-side of who won county contracts and who donated to the people who approved them.
exact.maui_contract_awards.html: The list of who got paid by Maui County, and for what.
exact.statewide_money_patterns.html: The flow of political money across Hawaiʻi since 2008 — who gives, and who receives.
exact.money_behind_officials.html: For each official, who funded them. Follow the money straight to the person.
exact.officials_scorecard.html: A simple report card for Mauiʻs officials — attendance, votes, and recusals.
exact.lege/legislator_scorecard.html: The same report card, for Hawaiʻiʻs state legislators.
exact.charter_application.html: How the 12 Stones charter lines up against real law, with the evidence shown beside it.
exact.commission_antitrust.html: A watch on commissions where a few hands may hold too much power.
exact.bill9/bill9_testimony_scan.html: Who testified on Bill 9, what they said, and how it ties to land, rent, and recovery money.
exact.parity_check.html: A pair is a promise that should answer itself — money in, value out. When a pair no longer answers, it shows up here.
exact.wildfire_recovery_watch.html: Where the Lahaina and Kula fire-recovery money is going, and who is rebuilding first.
exact.lobby_money_watch.html: Who pays lobbyists AND donates to the deciders — paying on both sides of the same table.
exact.jurisdictions.html: Every government we watch, from Maui to the Vatican. Pick yours.
exact.ka_leo_voice.html: Ka Leo means ʻthe voice.ʻ When a donation makes one voice louder than yours, we show it — so yours can be heard too.
exact.agenda_explainer.html: Share-ready cards for upcoming votes — what it is, who decides, and the moonʻs gentle suggestion for the day. Made to pass to a neighbor.
exact.agendas.html: Every governmentʻs upcoming votes in one place. Speak before the vote, not after.
exact.request_records.html: When a record is missing, this shows you how to ask for it — under each placeʻs own law — and send it back, so the gap becomes a fact on the ledger.
exact.sage_bridge.html: The civic world mapped onto the 54-node Sage journey — where things are in balance (pono), where a pair is broken (hewa), and what the moonʻs timing suggests.
exact.selfheal.html: The system checking its own promises — private kept private, public kept public, and the words held under community review.
exact.olelo_glossary.html: Every Hawaiian word we use, with a plain meaning, sent each week to ʻŌiwi resources at Maui County for verification. Offered with humility, not authority.
exact.n53_engine.html: The integrity engine that learns from past minutes, materials, and roll-call votes.
exact.testimony_record.html: Who spoke at meetings, what they said, and how it ties to money.
exact.testimony_money.html: Following the money by what people testified — advocacy, donations, and contracts side by side.

prefix.contracts_: Who won public contracts here, and for how much.
prefix.money_: Who gives political money here, and who receives it.
prefix.lobby_: Who lobbies here AND pays the deciders.
prefix.parity_: The pairs — money in, value out — that no longer answer here.
prefix.crosswalk_: How the 12 Stones charter lines up against this placeʻs real law.
prefix.agendas_: What is coming up for a vote here — so you can speak before it is decided, not after.
prefix.archive_: The saved record for this place — past agendas, minutes, and materials.
prefix.ka_leo_: Where donation money makes one voice louder here. Yours counts too.
prefix.subcontractors_: The companies working beneath the main contract holders here.

default: Part of the govOS civic record — public information, gathered so any Maui or Hawaiian person can see it plainly.
